headline: AI stocks tumble, tech turmoil continues
detail: Broad selling in AI and tech names is hitting the exact stocks that make up your book. PLTR and AVGO are already deep in the red, and a second straight day of sector-wide pressure raises the risk those stops get tested sooner than expected.

headline: OpenAI IPO delay clouds tech outlook
detail: A delayed OpenAI listing removes a near-term catalyst that was supporting bullish sentiment across AI-adjacent names. For high-multiple, story-driven stocks like PLTR, losing that narrative tailwind matters - it invites more selling from momentum traders.

headline: Friday set to be one of the biggest volume days of the year
detail: Wall Street's equivalent of roster-cut day hits Friday, meaning large institutional repositioning and potentially violent intraday swings. On margin, a high-volume flush day can blow through stops before you react - size and stop placement matter more than usual today.

headline: U.S. crude falls below $70, Hormuz shipping fragile
detail: Falling oil and Middle East shipping uncertainty add macro noise that can spook risk-off traders broadly. While not a direct hit on your chip and tech names, geopolitical flare-ups in that region historically trigger short-term tech selloffs as investors reduce overall risk exposure.
